duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: transferToUnoccupiedPlot never updates toilerState[tokenId].plotId to the new plotId, causing unstakeMunchable to clear the wrong (old) plot and permanently brick the new plot as occupied
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the identical missing state update (toilerState[tokenId].plotId not set in transferToUnoccupiedPlot) leading to permanent plot bricking on unstake. ECON-H001 selected for comprehensive attack economics, clear exploitation path, and CRITICAL severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: When a landlord's plot count decreases below a renter's plotId, _farmPlots sets timestamp to plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated which may be earlier than lastToilDate, causing an arithmetic underflow revert that permanently locks NFTs since unstake requires forceFarmPlots
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the same arithmetic underflow: (plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated - _toiler.lastToilDate) underflows when lastUpdated < lastToilDate in the dirty flag path, blocking all exit paths via forceFarmPlots. ECON-H002 selected for detailed preconditions, attack economics, and comprehensive impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: The schnibbles reward formula computes S*(1+B)/100 instead of S*(100+B)/100, systematically reducing all rewards by approximately 100x when finalBonus is near zero
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats identify the same mathematical error in _farmPlots where the entire expression (base + bonus) is divided by 100 instead of only the bonus portion. ECON2-H009 selected for CRITICAL severity, comprehensive mathematical analysis, and clear demonstration of the formula error's impact on all users.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: The dirty flag check uses strict less-than (_getNumPlots < plotId) instead of less-than-or-equal, allowing a renter on the boundary plotId to continue farming on a non-existent plot indefinitely
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the identical off-by-one error: 0-indexed plotIds mean plotId==numPlots is invalid, but the check N<N evaluates false, allowing unbacked yield accrual. ECON-H005 selected for HIGH severity with detailed economics showing continuous unbacked schnibbles generation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: The staking limit check uses > 10 instead of >= 10, allowing users to stake 11 NFTs instead of the intended maximum of 10
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats identify the same off-by-one error in stakeMunchable where length > 10 allows push at length==10, resulting in 11 staked NFTs. ECON-H011 selected for clear economic impact analysis quantifying the ~10% yield advantage.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: The reward calculation casts a potentially negative int256 result to uint256 without checking for negativity. When combined REALM_BONUSES and RARITY_BONUSES produce finalBonus <= -2 (due to the formula error), the cast wraps to near-2^256, minting astronomical schnibbles or reverting and locking NFTs
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 11 threats describe the same defect: negative finalBonus makes the int256 expression negative, and the uint256 cast either wraps to ~2^256 (infinite schnibbles) or reverts (NFT lock). ECON-H003 selected for CRITICAL severity, comprehensive analysis of the formula error interaction, and detailed explanation of how REALM_BONUSES intentionally includes negative values making this practically reachable.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: _reconfigure loads numeric configuration values (MIN_TAX_RATE, MAX_TAX_RATE, DEFAULT_TAX_RATE, BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE, PRICE_PER_PLOT) using StorageKeys designed for contract addresses, potentially loading wrong values or zeros. PRICE_PER_PLOT=0 causes division-by-zero DoS bricking all functions; wrong tax bounds break tax validation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 15 threats trace to the same root defect: _reconfigure uses StorageKey.LockManager/AccountManager/ClaimManager/MigrationManager/NFTOverlord for getUint calls when these keys are semantically contract address keys. TECH-H007 selected for CRITICAL severity and most comprehensive coverage of all 5 misconfigured parameters and their cascading effects (DoS, economic corruption, tax validation failure).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: If MAX_TAX_RATE exceeds 1e18 (due to config key mismatch loading address-as-uint), a landlord can set tax rate > 100%, causing schnibblesLandlord > schnibblesTotal in _farmPlots and an underflow revert on the renter's share, locking NFTs via forceFarmPlots
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same exploit chain: config mismatch → MAX_TAX_RATE > 1e18 → landlord sets extreme tax → schnibblesTotal - schnibblesLandlord underflows → _farmPlots reverts → NFTs locked. ECON2-H015 selected for HIGH severity with the most detailed code line references and complete exploitation chain analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: When the dirty flag triggers, _farmPlots uses plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated as the reward cutoff timestamp. If metadata was updated after plot invalidation, this grants excess rewards for the period between actual invalidation and the latest metadata update
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same timing vulnerability: lastUpdated reflects the latest metadata update rather than when the plot first became invalid, granting unearned rewards during the gap period. ECON-H006 selected for HIGH severity and detailed economic impact analysis of the timing gap exploitation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: updateTaxRate does not trigger farming for existing stakers before applying the new rate. While latestTaxRate provides single-period protection, the new rate takes effect on the next farm call, enabling landlord bait-and-switch tactics
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the same tax manipulation vector: landlord attracts stakers with low rate, raises to MAX after staking, exploiting the lack of forced farming on rate change. ECON-H007 selected for HIGH severity with comprehensive attack economics and victim impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Once toilerState[tokenId].dirty is set to true, the token is permanently skipped in _farmPlots (zero rewards) with no mechanism to clear the flag or transfer to a valid plot, requiring manual unstake and re-stake
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same one-way dirty flag mechanism where tokens get stuck in a zero-yield state with no recovery path other than full unstake. ECON-H010 selected for higher severity and more detailed analysis of landlord manipulation potential.
